reasoning: 4x/8x zoom inspection of the leather armor sprite. PASS criteria met: no sub-pixel blending, no AA edges, solid green body areas are clean, no color bleed between green and brown, semantic coherence is strong (readable chest armor with shoulder pauldrons and heart motif). MARGINAL issues: (1) Shoulder fur texture on left vs right pauldron shows minor pixel-level asymmetry — a handful of brown/orange pixels differ in placement between sides, suggesting generation rather than hand-mirroring. (2) Outline consistency around the fur boundary fluctuates between 0px and 1px gap in several spots on the upper shoulder fringe, where a clean hand-crafted sprite would maintain a uniform 1px dark outline. (3) The brown/orange fur pixel cluster has a slightly stochastic scatter pattern that feels more noise-sampled than deliberately placed — individual pixels at the fur tips appear random rather than following intentional dithering logic. Core form, palette discipline (3 discrete tones per region), and structural silhouette are solid. Not a clear fail, but the fur-boundary tells push this below a clean pass.
